title: Mussaenda hirsutissima
taxonomy: Mussaenda hirsutissima is classified in the family Rubiaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. This family includes over 600 genera and approximately 13,000 species. The genus Mussaenda, to which M. hirsutissima belongs, comprises around 120 species of flowering plants native to tropical regions. Mussaenda hirsutissima is a species of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family. Within this family and genus, M. hirsutissima is likely to be placed in a specific subsection or section, but this information may not be readily available.

intro: Mussaenda hirsutissima, also known as the hairy Mussaenda, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, which can aid in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Mussaenda hirsutissima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Mussaenda hirsutissima is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used as a raw material in the production of herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures.
